title: RuleFilletSettings Object
description: The settings for the rule fillet feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: setByAllEdges
methods.description: Method that adds an array of BRepFace and/or Feature objects to have all their edges filleted. Calling this method will set ruleType to RuleFilletRuleTypes.AllEdgesRuleFilletRuleType.

methods.name: setByBetweenFacesOrFeatures
methods.description: Method that adds two sets of BRepFace and/or Feature objects to have the edges between them filleted. Call this method will set ruleType to RuleFilletRuleTypes.BetweenFacesOrFeaturesRuleFilletRuleType.

methods.name: setToAsymmetric
methods.description: Changes the radius type to be asymmetric.

methods.name: setToConstantRadius
methods.description: Changes the radius type to be constant.

properties.name: asymmetricOffsetOne
properties.description: Gets the parameter controlling the first offset of this asymmetric rule fillet. This property will return null when isConstantRadius is true. To edit the offset, use properties on the parameter to change the value of the parameter.

properties.name: asymmetricOffsetTwo
properties.description: Gets the parameter controlling the second offset of this asymmetric rule fillet. This property will return null when isConstantRadius is true. To edit the offset, use properties on the parameter to change the value of the parameter.

properties.name: facesOrFeatures
properties.description: Gets an array of BRepFace and/or Feature objects that have all their edges filleted. This returns an empty array if ruleType is not RuleFilletRuleTypes.AllEdgesRuleFilletRuleType.   To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True).

properties.name: facesOrFeaturesOne
properties.description: Gets the first array of BRepFace and/or Feature objects for between faces/features rule fillet. This returns an empty array if ruleType is not RuleFilletRuleTypes.BetweenFacesOrFeaturesRuleFilletRuleType.   To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True).

properties.name: facesOrFeaturesTwo
properties.description: Gets the second array of BRepFace and/or Feature objects for between faces/features rule fillet. This returns an empty array if ruleType is not RuleFilletRuleTypes.BetweenFacesOrFeaturesRuleFilletRuleType.   To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True).

properties.name: isConstantRadius
properties.description: Gets and sets if the rule fillet is a constant or asymmetric radius type.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: radius
properties.description: Gets the parameter controlling the radius of a constant radius rule fillet. This property will return null when isConstantRadius is false. To edit the radius, use properties on the parameter to change the value of the parameter.

properties.name: ruleType
properties.description: Gets the rule type for the rule fillet.

properties.name: topologyType
properties.description: Gets and sets the topology type of the rule fillet.   To set this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True).

asymmetricOffsetOne.title: RuleFilletSettings.asymmetricOffsetOne Property
asymmetricOffsetOne.description: Gets the parameter controlling the first offset of this asymmetric rule fillet. This property will return null when isConstantRadius is true. To edit the offset, use properties on the parameter to change the value of the parameter.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = ruleFilletSettings_var.asymmetricOffsetOne

asymmetricOffsetTwo.title: RuleFilletSettings.asymmetricOffsetTwo Property
asymmetricOffsetTwo.description: Gets the parameter controlling the second offset of this asymmetric rule fillet. This property will return null when isConstantRadius is true. To edit the offset, use properties on the parameter to change the value of the parameter.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = ruleFilletSettings_var.asymmetricOffsetTwo

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = ruleFilletSettings_var.topologyType

# Set the value of the property.
ruleFilletSettings_var.topologyType = propertyValue

classType.title: RuleFilletSettings.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

setByAllEdges.title: RuleFilletSettings.setByAllEdges Method
setByAllEdges.description: Method that adds an array of BRepFace and/or Feature objects to have all their edges filleted. Calling this method will set ruleType to RuleFilletRuleTypes.AllEdgesRuleFilletRuleType.
setByAllEdges.return_description: Returns true if the operation was successful.

parameters.name: facesOrFeatures
parameters.description: Input faces and/or features to have all their edges to be filleted.

setByBetweenFacesOrFeatures.title: RuleFilletSettings.setByBetweenFacesOrFeatures Method
setByBetweenFacesOrFeatures.description: Method that adds two sets of BRepFace and/or Feature objects to have the edges between them filleted. Call this method will set ruleType to RuleFilletRuleTypes.BetweenFacesOrFeaturesRuleFilletRuleType.
setByBetweenFacesOrFeatures.return_description: Returns true if the operation was successful.

parameters.name: facesOrFeaturesOne
parameters.description: Input first array of BRepFace and/or Feature objects.

parameters.name: facesOrFeaturesTwo
parameters.description: Input second array of BRepFace and/or Feature objects.

setToAsymmetric.title: RuleFilletSettings.setToAsymmetric Method
setToAsymmetric.description: Changes the radius type to be asymmetric.

parameters.name: offsetOne
parameters.description: Input ValueInput object that defines the first offset of the asymmetric rule fillet. If the ValueInput uses a real value then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in") or if no units are specified it is interpreted using the current document units for length.

parameters.name: offsetTwo
parameters.description: Input ValueInput object that defines the second offset of the asymmetric rule fillet. If the ValueInput uses a real value then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in") or if no units are specified it is interpreted using the current document units for length.

setToConstantRadius.title: RuleFilletSettings.setToConstantRadius Method
setToConstantRadius.description: Changes the radius type to be constant.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: Input ValueInput object that defines the radius of the rule fillet. If the ValueInput uses a real value then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in") or if no units are specified it is interpreted using the current document units for length.
